segments.text: O God, why have you cast us off forever? Why does your anger smoke against the sheep of your pasture?
segments.speaker: Asaph

footnotes: Maschil: or, A Psalm for Asaph to give instruction

segments.text: Remember your congregation, which you have purchased of old, the rod of your inheritance, which you have redeemed—this Mount Zion where you have dwelt.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Lift up your feet to the perpetual desolations, the enemy has destroyed everything in the sanctuary.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Your enemies roar in the midst of your meeting place;
they set up their own signs for signs.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: They seem like men who lift up axes among the thick trees.
And now they break down its carved work all at once with hatchets and hammers.
They have cast fire into your sanctuary, they have defiled the dwelling place of your name to the ground.

footnotes: They have cast your sanctuary unto the fire

segments.text: They say in their heart, 'Let us destroy them altogether.' They have burned up all the meeting places of God in the land.
We do not see our signs; there is no longer any prophet, nor is there among us any who knows how long.
O God, how long shall the adversary reproach? Shall the enemy blaspheme your name forever?
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Why do you withdraw your hand, even your right hand? Pluck it out of your bosom and destroy them.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: For God is my King of old, working salvation in the midst of the earth.
You divided the sea by your strength; you broke the heads of the sea dragons in the waters.
You crushed the heads of Leviathan, and gave him as food to the people inhabiting the wilderness.
You split open the fountain and the flood; you dried up mighty rivers.

footnotes: mighty rivers: Heb. rivers of strength

segments.text: The day is yours, the night also is yours; you have prepared the light and the sun.
You have set all the borders of the earth; you have made summer and winter.
Remember this, that the enemy has reproached, O LORD, and that a foolish people has blasphemed your name.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Oh, do not deliver the life of your turtledove to the wild beast; do not forget the life of your afflicted forever.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Have respect to the covenant; for the dark places of the earth are full of the habitations of cruelty.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Oh, do not let the oppressed return ashamed; let the poor and needy praise your name.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Arise, O God, plead your own cause; remember how the foolish man reproaches you daily.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Do not forget the voice of your enemies; the tumult of those who rise up against you increases continually.
segments.speaker: Asaph
